violations.description: Preventing contamination by employees. — --Employee observed handling ready-to-eat food * with bare hands. Witnessed employee making a salad to go. He was cutting vegetables and placing them on the salad with his bare hands. Need to use gloves, or utensils.

Preventing contamination from equipment, utensils, and wiping cloths. — --Cloth for wiping food spills was not stored in sanitizing solution (at proper concentration) between uses. Wet wiping cloths were stored on the prep counter. Witnessed employee rinsing towels in the kitchen hand sink and then wiping food contact surfaces. Wiping cloths must be stored in sanitizer solution.--In-use utensil was not properly stored during pauses in food preparation or dispensing. Rice scoop and bowl was being stored in a container of room temp water by the rice cooker.
Cleaning frequency of equipment and utensils. — --Ventilation hood system was not cleaned properly and/or as frequently as required. Filters in the hood system over the cooktop need to be cleaned.

Manual cleaning and sanitizing requirements. — --Large equipment/utensil was not sanitized in place as required. Cutting boards on the prep counter were wiped with a cloth that had was just rinsed in the hand sink. No sanitizer was used to clean these food contact surfaces.

Handwashing lavatories. — --Handwashing lavatory was used for purpose other than handwashing. Employees were using the hand sink in the kitchen to rinse wiping cloths. Hand sinks can only be used for hand washing.

Design and construction of floors, walls, and ceilings. — --Floors, walls, and/or ceiling were not constructed or designed to be smooth, nonabsorbent, and easily cleanable. The top of the wall in the dish area is bare sheet rock. The wood around the door is bare wood. These are not cleanable surfaces.

Cleaning and maintenance of floors, walls and ceilings. — --Physical facilities (e.g. floors, walls, ceiling etc…) were not maintained in good repair. The paint surface is coming off the floor in the kitchen, exposed concrete.

Employee dressing areas and lockers. — --Employees' clothing/possessions were in an area where contamination of food, equipment, utensils, linens or single-service items may occur. Employee speakers and phone were placed on the counter of the cooler across from the cooking equipment.
